document_title: Model Work Health and Safety Act
notes: Model (TEMPLATE) text maintained by Safe Work Australia. NOT enforceable law in itself — binding only where a jurisdiction separately enacts it. SWA enforces nothing. Underlies the harmonised WHS framework adopted by all AU jurisdictions EXCEPT WA (OSH Act 1984; WA's WHS Act 2020 is a partial/variant adoption); timing/variations differ by jurisdiction. Amendments to the model WHS Act (incl. incident-notification changes) published 5 Dec 2025 — set currency_version_date 2025-12. /doc/ landing blocks automated fetch; confirmed via SWA search snippet.  [gap-review] Model WHS Act — version 5 Dec 2025 (model text; no statutory instrument id).

document_title: AS ISO 31000:2018 Risk management — Guidelines
ingest_caveat: Copyright Standards Australia (ISO-derived) — purchase-only standard; full text must NOT be ingested. Catalogue/citation metadata only.
notes: ADDED by verifier — missing core cross-cutting standard. Identical adoption of ISO 31000:2018 'Risk management — Guidelines', approved 19 Sept 2018, published 30 Oct 2018; supersedes AS/NZS ISO 31000:2009. AU-only adoption (NOT AS/NZS — SA/SNZ decided to publish as an Australian Standard). 8 principles; foundational for the risk-management process underpinning all WHS hazard assessment. Designation/title/date CONFIRMED via search snippet (standards.org.au catalogue + Commonwealth Fraud Prevention Centre). Voluntary; purchase only.

document_title: R v LH Holding Management Pty Ltd & Hanna [2024] VSC 90
notes: Victoria's FIRST workplace (industrial) manslaughter sentencing under s 39G(1) Occupational Health and Safety Act 2004 (Vic) — VIC is a NON-harmonised jurisdiction (OHS Act 2004, not model WHS). Company fined $1.3M ($2M before guilty-plea discount); sole director Laith Hanna convicted (officer liability s 144) and placed on 2-year Community Correction Order (200 hrs unpaid work + forklift operation course); $120k compensation ordered. Forklift/jib load fatality. Citation/facts CONFIRMED via search snippet (Norton Rose Fulbright, WorkSafe Vic). hazard_category changed GENERAL->PLANT_EQUIPMENT (forklift). AustLII 403s automated fetch -> url_verified=false; assembler should confirm exact AustLII/Jade path. Tier 3 / borderline for this node but retained as landmark.

document_title: The Queen v Brisbane Auto Recycling Pty Ltd & Ors [2020] QDC 113
notes: Australia's FIRST industrial manslaughter conviction. Company convicted of industrial manslaughter (s 34C WHS Act 2011 (Qld)) and fined $3M; two directors (Hussaini, Karimi) pleaded guilty to Category 1 reckless conduct (s 31) and each received 10 months imprisonment wholly suspended (NOT 'convicted of Category 1' only — corrected from enumerator note). Forklift fatality; no safety systems, unlicensed operator. URL CONFIRMED via search snippet (Queensland Judgments caselaw). PDF mirror: archive.sclqld.org.au/qjudgment/2020/QDC20-113.pdf. NOTE: court decisions are Tier 3 / borderline for a 'guidance + standards' node but retained as landmark cross-cutting case law.

notes: Doctrinally distinct landmark from QLD-CASE-001 (corporate guilty plea). Medium-neutral citation VERIFY. Admitted on the CLAUDE.md s9 landmark-stretch basis.

document_title: Work Health and Safety Amendment (Safer Workplaces) Act 2024 (Tas)
notes: Industrial-manslaughter reform amending the principal WHS Act 2012. Title and citation verified via legislation.tas.gov.au (asmade) — 'Work Health and Safety Amendment (Safer Workplaces) Act 2024', Act No. 15 of 2024, Royal Assent 2 Oct 2024. Inserts the industrial manslaughter offence (max 21 yrs imprisonment for individuals; $18m for bodies corporate; no 2-yr limitation period). CORRECTION: the enacted Act is the Government's 'Safer Workplaces' Bill (24 of 2024), NOT the separate Private Member's 'WHS Amendment (Industrial Manslaughter) Bill 2024 (15 of 2024)' — the enumerator conflated the two bills. Amending Act — its provisions are consolidated into act-2012-001.

document_title: Work Health and Safety (Crystalline Silica Substances) Amendment Regulations 2024 (Tas)
notes: Statutory Rules 2024 No. 36. Title verified via legislation.tas.gov.au and AustLII. Amends Chapter 8A of the WHS Regulations 2022 — renames it 'Crystalline Silica' and inserts crystalline silica substance (CSS, ≥1% crystalline silica by weight) controls (high-risk process identification, silica risk control plan, training, air monitoring). CORRECTION: changed URL from the PDF (asmade pdf) to the HTML asmade view for cleaner ingestion; both resolve. Amending instrument — substantive content consolidated in sr-2022-109.

document_title: Health and Safety at Work Amendment Bill 2026
notes: BILL, NOT ENACTED as of 2026-06-30. Bill URL exists on legislation.govt.nz. Education and Workforce Select Committee reported back 16 Jun 2026 recommending (by majority) the Bill pass with amendments; most provisions PROPOSED to commence 1 Nov 2026 (a future date). One secondary search snippet loosely described it as 'formally enacted' but that is inconsistent with the 16 Jun 2026 report-back and a proposed 1 Nov 2026 commencement; treated as NOT in force. Amends HSWA 2015, WorkSafe New Zealand Act 2013 and the GRWM Regulations 2016. Included for currency awareness only; do NOT treat as in-force law. VERIFY enactment status against legislation.govt.nz / bills.parliament.nz before relying on it.  [gap-review] Reclassified from act to bill (not yet enacted as of 2026-06-30).

document_title: Confined spaces: planning entry and working safely in a confined space
notes: CORRECTION: page fetched OK (200) but this is NOT a full 'Good Practice Guidelines' — it is a short WorkSafe QUICK GUIDE (~181KB PDF) summarising the standard. WorkSafe endorses AS 2865 Confined spaces as the current state of knowledge and states the quick guide is not a substitute for the standard. Removed the '– Good Practice Guidelines' suffix from the title to avoid overstating its status. Mapped CONFINED_SPACES. Note AS 2865 itself is a third-party AS/NZS standard (copyright Standards Australia/NZ) and is NOT covered by the crown-copyright-open licence — do not ingest the standard text.
